cases.title: Create Grid bot with valid default values
cases.description: Verify a Futures Grid bot is created successfully when all required fields are filled with valid values matching AC defaults.
cases.preconditions: User is logged in. Brokerage connected. Sufficient margin available.
cases.rawTestData: symbol: BTCUSDT | lowerPrice: 50000 | upperPrice: 80000 | numberOfGrids: 10 | quantityPerGrid: 0.001 | gridMode: Neutral | liquidateWhenOutOfRange: OFF
steps: Navigate to Bot Trading → Futures Grid
Enter all fields with the above values
cases.expected: Bot created successfully. Grid orders placed at equal price intervals between 50,000 and 80,000. Bot visible in active bots with status Running.

cases.title: lowerPrice equal to upperPrice — validation error
cases.description: Verify the system rejects the configuration when lowerPrice equals upperPrice, as this produces a zero-width grid with no valid levels.
cases.preconditions: User is on Grid bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error displayed: 'Lower price must be less than upper price.' Bot not created. Start Bot button is disabled.

cases.title: lowerPrice greater than upperPrice — validation error
cases.description: Verify the system rejects inverted price range (lowerPrice > upperPrice), which is explicitly prohibited by the AC (lowerPrice < upperPrice).
cases.preconditions: User is on Grid bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Lower price must be less than upper price.' Bot not created. Field highlighted. Start Bot button is disabled.

cases.title: lowerPrice = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify lowerPrice = 0 is rejected. AC specifies lowerPrice must be > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on Grid bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Lower price must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.Start Bot button is disabled.

cases.title: quantityPerGrid below minimum lot size — validation error
cases.description: Verify the system rejects quantityPerGrid below the brokerage's minimum lot size or minimum order size, as required by the AC (quantityPerGrid ≥ lotSize and ≥ minOrderSize).
cases.preconditions: User connected to Binance Futures. BTCUSDT min lot size: 0.001.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Quantity per grid must be at least [lotSize].' Specific minimum displayed. Bot not created.Start Bot button is disabled.

cases.title: quantityPerGrid = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify quantityPerGrid = 0 is rejected. AC specifies quantityPerGrid > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on Grid bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Quantity per grid must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.Start Bot button is disabled.

cases.title: gridMode = Neutral — places buy and sell orders on both sides of current price
cases.description: Verify Neutral mode creates a symmetric grid: buy orders below current price AND sell orders above current price.
cases.preconditions: Grid bot created and running. BTCUSDT current price is within the configured range.
cases.rawTestData: gridMode: Neutral | lowerPrice: 50000 | upperPrice: 80000 | current price: ~71000
steps: Create Grid bot with gridMode = Neutral
Check open orders on the brokerage
cases.expected: Open orders contain both limit buy orders at levels below current price AND limit sell orders at levels above current price. Number of orders on each side corresponds to grid levels.

cases.title: gridMode = Long — places only buy orders below current price
cases.description: Verify Long mode creates a directional grid with only buy orders, consistent with a long-biased strategy. No short/sell orders should be placed.
cases.preconditions: Grid bot configured with gridMode = Long.
steps: Create Grid bot with gridMode = Long
cases.expected: Only limit buy orders exist in the open orders list. No sell orders placed at grid levels above current price. Grid is directional long.

cases.title: liquidateWhenOutOfRange = ON — position liquidated when price exits grid range
cases.description: Verify that when the toggle is ON, the bot automatically liquidates the position if price moves outside the defined upper or lower boundary.
cases.preconditions: Grid bot running with liquidateWhenOutOfRange = ON. Price is within range.
cases.rawTestData: lowerPrice: 50000 | upperPrice: 80000 | liquidateWhenOutOfRange: ON | Simulate price dropping to 49000
steps: Simulate or wait for price to drop below lowerPrice (49,000)
cases.expected: Bot automatically liquidates open position when price breaches lowerPrice. All open grid orders cancelled. Position = 0. Event logged in bot activity.

cases.title: liquidateWhenOutOfRange = OFF — position retained when price exits range
cases.description: Verify that when the toggle is OFF (default), the bot pauses grid activity but does NOT liquidate when price exits the range. User retains the position.
cases.preconditions: Grid bot running with liquidateWhenOutOfRange = OFF. Price exits range.
cases.rawTestData: lowerPrice: 50000 | upperPrice: 80000 | liquidateWhenOutOfRange: OFF | Price drops to 49000
cases.expected: Bot stops placing new grid orders outside the range. Open position (if any) is NOT automatically closed. User retains their BTC position. Bot status may show 'Paused' or 'Out of Range'.

cases.title: maxNearestLevels validated — must be a positive integer less than 10
cases.description: Verify the AC rule: maxNearestLevels > 0, must be an integer. Per spec note: temporarily fixed at < 10. Entering 0 or ≥ 10 should fail validation.
cases.preconditions: User is on Grid bot creation screen.
cases.rawTestData: Test A: maxNearestLevels = 0 | Test B: maxNearestLevels = 10 | Test C: maxNearestLevels = 1.5 (decimal)
cases.expected: Test A: Error 'Must be > 0'. Test B: Error 'Must be less than 10'. Test C: Error 'Must be a whole number'. None of the three configurations create a bot.

cases.title: profitSpreadMultiplier = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify profitSpreadMultiplier = 0 is rejected. AC specifies it must be > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on Grid bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Profit spread multiplier must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.Start Bot button is disabled.

cases.title: profitSpreadMultiplier = 0.5 — sell order placed at half grid step (tighter grid)
cases.description: Verify that with multiplier = 0.5, the sell order for each filled buy is placed at half the standard grid step above the buy price — reducing profit target per cycle.
cases.preconditions: Grid bot running. profitSpreadMultiplier = 0.5. Grid step = $3,000 (30,000 ÷ 10 grids).
steps: Create and start bot with multiplier = 0.5
Wait for or simulate a buy fill at a grid level (e.g., $65,000)
Check the corresponding sell order price
cases.expected: Sell order placed at $65,000 + ($3,000 × 0.5) = $66,500. Not at the standard next grid level ($68,000). Confirms multiplier scales the sell target correctly.

cases.title: Create DCA bot with valid configuration — all fields
cases.description: Verify a DCA bot is created successfully when all required and optional fields are set to valid values per AC.
cases.preconditions: User is logged in. Brokerage connected.
cases.rawTestData: symbol: BTCUSDT | direction: Long | baseOrderQuantity: 0.01 | dcaOrderQuantity: 0.005 | maxDcaOrders: 5 | priceDeviation: 1% | takeProfit: 1.5% | stopLoss: 10% | volumeScale: 1 | priceDeviationScale: 1
steps: Fill all fields with above values
cases.expected: Bot created. Base order placed immediately at market. System schedules up to 5 safety orders at 1% price deviation intervals below entry.

cases.title: baseOrderQuantity = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify baseOrderQuantity = 0 is rejected. AC requires > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Base order quantity must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: dcaOrderQuantity = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify dcaOrderQuantity = 0 is rejected. AC requires > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'DCA order quantity must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: priceDeviation = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify priceDeviation = 0 is rejected. AC requires > 0. A zero deviation would place all DCA orders at the same price as the entry, making them non-functional.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Price deviation must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: maxDcaOrders = 0 — valid, bot runs with base order only
cases.description: Verify that maxDcaOrders = 0 is accepted (AC specifies ≥ 0). The bot should place only the base order and never place any safety/DCA orders.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
steps: After base order fills, observe if any DCA orders are placed
cases.expected: Bot created successfully. Base order placed. No DCA/safety orders are ever placed regardless of how much price falls. Bot waits for TP or SL on the base position only.

cases.title: takeProfit field left empty — system transmits 0, bot runs without take profit
cases.description: Verify that leaving takeProfit blank results in the system sending 0 to the backend (per AC: 'not required, if user does not enter, send 0'). The bot should run indefinitely without a TP trigger.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
steps: Monitor bot — confirm no TP order is placed after entry
Inspect bot config in Details view
cases.expected: Bot created without error. Details view shows takeProfit = 0 or 'None'. No take profit order is placed on the brokerage. Position stays open until manually closed or SL triggers.

cases.title: stopLoss = 0 — valid, bot runs without stop loss
cases.description: Verify stopLoss = 0 is accepted (AC: ≥ 0). The bot should have no automatic stop loss, meaning position stays open even if price falls significantly.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
steps: Simulate large price drop past DCA levels
Check if any stop loss order fires
cases.expected: Bot created without error. No stop loss order placed. Position remains open after large price drop. User is exposed to full downside — this is expected and per spec.

cases.title: volumeScale = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify volumeScale = 0 is rejected. AC specifies > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Volume scale must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: priceDeviationScale = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify priceDeviationScale = 0 is rejected. AC specifies > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on DCA bot creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Price deviation scale must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: DCA bot stops placing orders after maxDcaOrders is reached
cases.description: Verify the bot places exactly maxDcaOrders safety orders and then stops — no additional orders are placed even if price continues to fall.
cases.preconditions: DCA bot running. maxDcaOrders = 3. Price falls through all 3 DCA levels.
steps: Wait or simulate price falling through all 3 DCA levels
Simulate price falling a further 2% below the 3rd DCA level
cases.expected: Exactly 3 DCA orders placed (one at each deviation level). No 4th order placed when price falls further. Total position = base + 3× DCA orders. Bot waits for TP or SL.

cases.title: volumeScale > 1 — each subsequent DCA order quantity is scaled up correctly
cases.description: Verify that with volumeScale = 2, each DCA order quantity doubles the previous one, as the multiplier compounds across safety orders.
cases.preconditions: DCA bot running. volumeScale = 2. dcaOrderQuantity = 0.01.
steps: Start and let first DCA order fill
Check quantities of each DCA order in order history
cases.expected: DCA order 1: 0.01 BTC. DCA order 2: 0.02 BTC (×2). DCA order 3: 0.04 BTC (×2). Quantities compound correctly with the scale factor.

cases.title: priceDeviationScale > 1 — DCA levels widen with each successive order
cases.description: Verify that with priceDeviationScale = 2, each successive DCA level is placed at a greater percentage deviation from the previous — spacing between levels grows.
cases.preconditions: DCA bot. priceDeviation = 1%. priceDeviationScale = 2. Entry at $71,000.
steps: Check prices of DCA orders 1, 2, and 3 in open orders
cases.expected: DCA level 1: 1% below entry = $70,290. DCA level 2: 2% below level 1 = ~$68,876. DCA level 3: 4% below level 2 = ~$66,121. Deviations compound — each level is progressively further apart.

cases.title: Create TWAP bot with valid configuration
cases.description: Verify a TWAP bot is created successfully with all required fields set to valid values per AC.
cases.preconditions: User is logged in. Brokerage connected with sufficient margin.
cases.rawTestData: symbol: BTCUSDT | side: Buy | totalQuantity: 0.1 | durationMinutes: 60 | sliceIntervalSeconds: 30 | maxDeviationPercentAbove: 0.3% | maxDeviationPercentBelow: 0.3% | usePostOnly: true | marketExecuteUnfilled: false
steps: Enter all fields with above values
cases.expected: Bot created. System calculates number of slices = (60 × 60) ÷ 30 = 120. Each slice quantity ≈ 0.1 ÷ 120 ≈ 0.000833 BTC. First limit order placed at market open.

cases.title: totalQuantity = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify totalQuantity = 0 is rejected. AC requires > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on TWAP creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Total quantity must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: durationMinutes = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify durationMinutes = 0 is rejected. AC requires > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on TWAP creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Duration must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: sliceIntervalSeconds = 0 — validation error
cases.description: Verify sliceIntervalSeconds = 0 is rejected. AC requires > 0.
cases.preconditions: User is on TWAP creation screen.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Slice interval must be greater than 0.' Bot not created.

cases.title: sliceIntervalSeconds > total duration in seconds — only 1 slice placed
cases.description: Verify edge case where sliceIntervalSeconds exceeds the total duration (durationMinutes × 60). System should place exactly 1 slice for the full quantity.
cases.preconditions: User is on TWAP creation screen.
cases.expected: Bot places exactly 1 order for the full totalQuantity (0.1 BTC). No subsequent slices. Execution completes after the first slice. Alternatively, a validation warning is shown that only 1 slice will execute.

cases.title: usePostOnly = true — limit orders are post-only; immediate-fill orders are cancelled
cases.description: Verify that with usePostOnly = true, all slice orders are sent as Post-Only to the brokerage. If a slice order would immediately match (become taker), it is cancelled and not executed as a market order.
cases.preconditions: TWAP bot running. usePostOnly = true. marketExecuteUnfilled = false.

testData._2: Market is thin; slice order would cross the spread

cases.rawTestData: usePostOnly: true | marketExecuteUnfilled: false | Market is thin; slice order would cross the spread
steps: Simulate a slice interval where the order price would immediately fill (crosses spread)
Check order execution in Orders tab
cases.expected: Slice order is cancelled (not filled) when it would have been a taker. A new post-only order is placed at the next interval. No market orders sent to brokerage. Brokerage confirms Post-Only flag on all orders.

cases.title: marketExecuteUnfilled = true — unfilled limit order converted to market at interval end
cases.description: Verify that with marketExecuteUnfilled = true, any slice order that is still open at the end of its interval is automatically converted to a market order to ensure execution.
cases.preconditions: TWAP bot running. marketExecuteUnfilled = true.

testData._2: Limit order not filled within 30 seconds

cases.rawTestData: sliceIntervalSeconds: 30 | marketExecuteUnfilled: true | Limit order not filled within 30 seconds
steps: Start TWAP bot with marketExecuteUnfilled = true
Wait for a slice interval where the limit order does not fill within 30 seconds
Check order events at the 30-second mark
cases.expected: At the 30-second mark, the unfilled limit order is converted to a market order and executed. Order Events tab shows: limit order → cancelled → market order placed and filled. Total quantity for the slice is executed.

cases.title: marketExecuteUnfilled = false — unfilled limit order is cancelled, not market-executed
cases.description: Verify that with marketExecuteUnfilled = false (default), an unfilled slice limit order is simply cancelled at interval end. The quantity for that slice is NOT executed as a market order.
cases.preconditions: TWAP bot running. marketExecuteUnfilled = false.

testData._2: Limit order not filled within 30 seconds

cases.rawTestData: sliceIntervalSeconds: 30 | marketExecuteUnfilled: false | Limit order not filled within 30 seconds
steps: Start TWAP bot with marketExecuteUnfilled = false
Wait for an unfilled slice limit order at interval end
cases.expected: Unfilled limit order is cancelled at interval end. No market order is placed. That slice's quantity is skipped (or redistributed to future slices, per implementation). No unintended market execution.

cases.title: Create Portfolio Rebalancing with valid config — RegularIntervals mode
cases.description: Verify a Portfolio Rebalancing bot is created successfully with at least 1 asset and RegularIntervals scheduling.
cases.preconditions: User is logged in. Account holds at least 1 asset.
cases.rawTestData: portfolioTargets: [{symbol: BTCUSDT, weight: 50%}, {symbol: ETHUSDT, weight: 50%}] | scheduling.mode: RegularIntervals | scheduling.frequency: EveryDay | scheduling.time: 08:00:00
steps: Navigate to Bot Trading → Portfolio Rebalancing
Set mode to RegularIntervals, frequency EveryDay, time 08:00:00
cases.expected: Bot created. System schedules daily rebalance at 08:00. At trigger time, bot calculates current allocation vs target and places buy/sell orders to reach 50/50 split.

cases.title: No asset target added — validation error (at least 1 required)
cases.description: Verify the bot cannot be created without at least one portfolio target asset, per AC: 'At least 1' required.
cases.preconditions: User is on Portfolio Rebalancing creation screen. No assets added.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Please add at least one portfolio target asset.' Bot not created.

cases.title: scheduling.mode = SpecificTimes without times[] — validation error
cases.description: Verify that selecting SpecificTimes mode without providing at least one time entry is rejected, per AC: 'Required when mode = SpecificTimes'.
cases.preconditions: User has selected SpecificTimes as scheduling mode. No times added.
cases.expected: Validation error: 'Please add at least one scheduled time for Specific Times mode.' Bot not created.

cases.title: scheduling.time in invalid format — validation error
cases.description: Verify that a time value not matching HH:mm:ss format is rejected by the system.
cases.preconditions: User is on Portfolio Rebalancing creation screen.
cases.rawTestData: scheduling.time: '8:00' (invalid — missing seconds) | also test: '25:00:00' (invalid hour)
cases.expected: Both inputs rejected with validation error: 'Time must be in HH:mm:ss format.' For '25:00:00', additional error: 'Invalid time value.' Bot not created in either case.

cases.title: scheduling.mode = SpecificTimes with multiple times — rebalances at each scheduled time
cases.description: Verify that adding multiple times in SpecificTimes mode causes the bot to trigger a rebalance at each listed time, not just the first one.
cases.preconditions: Portfolio Rebalancing bot running. scheduling.mode = SpecificTimes. Multiple times configured.
steps: Create bot with SpecificTimes and three scheduled times
Monitor bot activity log over a full day
Verify rebalance events at each scheduled time
cases.expected: Rebalance executed at 08:00, 14:00, and 20:00. Three rebalance events logged in activity. No rebalance occurs outside these times. Each event calculates and trades to target allocation independently.

cases.title: scheduling.frequency = EveryDay fires exactly once per day
cases.description: Verify RegularIntervals with EveryDay frequency triggers exactly one rebalance per day at the specified time — not more, not less.
cases.preconditions: Portfolio Rebalancing bot running. mode = RegularIntervals, frequency = EveryDay, time = 09:00:00.
cases.expected: Exactly 2 rebalance events logged over 48 hours (one per day at 09:00). No extra triggers between scheduled times. No missed triggers.

cases.title: Switch chart to 15m interval
cases.description: Verify chart reloads with 15-minute candles when 15m is selected.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page with a pair loaded.
steps: Click '15m' in the interval selector toolbar
cases.expected: Chart reloads with 15-minute candlesticks. MA values (MA5, MA10, MA30, MA60) update accordingly.

cases.title: Switch chart to 1H interval
cases.description: Verify chart reloads with 1-hour candles.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page.
cases.expected: Chart reloads with 1-hour candles. Time axis and volume bars update.

cases.title: Switch chart to 4H, 1D, 1W intervals
cases.description: Verify chart correctly renders when switching to 4H, 1D, and 1W intervals.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page.
cases.expected: Each interval loads correct candle data. Historical range increases with longer intervals. No rendering errors.

cases.title: Selected interval persists after switching trading pair
cases.description: Verify the selected interval is kept when user switches to a different trading pair.
cases.preconditions: User has selected 4H interval.
cases.rawTestData: Current pair: BTC/USDT | Interval: 4H | New pair: ETH/USDT
steps: Switch trading pair to ETH/USDT via pair selector
cases.expected: ETH/USDT chart loads at 4H. Interval does not reset to default after pair change.

cases.title: Search and select a trading pair
cases.description: Verify user can search for a pair and switch to it on the chart.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page. Multiple pairs available.
steps: Click the pair selector (e.g. BTC/USDT dropdown at top left)
cases.expected: Chart switches to ETH/USDT. Header shows correct price, 24h change, 24h high, 24h low, and volume.

cases.title: Filter trading pairs by brokerage
cases.description: Verify pair list correctly filters when user selects a specific brokerage.
cases.preconditions: User is on pair selection screen. Multiple brokerages connected (Binance, OKX, Bybit, etc.).
cases.expected: Only Binance pairs are shown. Pairs from other brokerages (OKX, KuCoin, etc.) are hidden.

cases.title: Search for non-existent trading pair
cases.description: Verify appropriate empty state when search returns no results.
cases.preconditions: User is on pair search screen.
cases.expected: No results shown. Empty state message displayed such as 'No pairs found'.

cases.title: Switch between Original and Trading View modes
cases.description: Verify user can toggle between 'Original' and 'Trading View' chart modes.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page with chart loaded.
steps: Click 'Trading View' button in chart toolbar
cases.expected: TradingView mode renders correctly with full feature set. Switching back restores original chart without data loss.

cases.title: MA indicators display and calculate correctly
cases.description: Verify MA5, MA10, MA30, MA60 lines are visible and show correct values on the chart.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page. BTC/USDT loaded at 15m.
steps: Check MA lines visually on chart
Verify MA values shown in header bar match last candle
cases.expected: MA5, MA10, MA30, MA60 lines drawn on chart. Values in header (e.g. MA5: 10.30) are consistent with visible data.

cases.title: Order book updates in real time
cases.description: Verify the order book right panel refreshes live with new market orders.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page with BTC/USDT loaded. WebSocket active.
cases.rawTestData: Pair: BTC/USDT | Live market data streaming
steps: Observe order book (right panel) for 30 seconds
cases.expected: Order book rows refresh continuously. Ask/bid prices, quantities, and order sizes update without page reload.

cases.title: Volume chart visible and correct
cases.description: Verify volume sub-chart below main chart shows correct bars per candle period.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page.
cases.rawTestData: Pair: BTC/USDT | Volume MAs: MA5, MA10, MA20 shown
steps: Observe the volume sub-chart below main chart
cases.expected: Volume bars visible and aligned with candles. Volume MA lines (MA5, MA10, MA20) rendered correctly.

cases.title: Expand chart to fullscreen
cases.description: Verify chart expands to fullscreen when fullscreen icon is clicked.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page.
steps: Click the fullscreen icon in the chart toolbar
cases.expected: Chart expands to full browser window. Other panels hidden. Pressing Esc or clicking icon again returns to normal layout.

cases.title: Enable AI indicator on chart
cases.description: Verify AI signals overlay appears on chart when AI button is activated.
cases.preconditions: User is on Trading page. Chart loaded.
steps: Click the 'AI' button in the top-right toolbar
cases.expected: AI signals (buy/sell indicators or prediction zones) appear on the chart. AI button is highlighted as active.

cases.title: Disable AI indicator
cases.description: Verify AI overlay is removed when the AI button is toggled off.
cases.preconditions: AI indicator is currently enabled on the chart.
steps: Click the AI button again to toggle it off
cases.expected: AI overlay removed from chart. Chart returns to clean state. AI button returns to inactive.

cases.title: AI indicator recalculates when interval changes
cases.description: Verify AI signals update when the chart interval is switched.
cases.preconditions: AI indicator is enabled. User changes interval from 1H to 4H.
cases.expected: AI signals recalculate for the 4H interval. Overlay updates automatically without needing to re-enable.

cases.title: Search for trading pair by name
cases.description: Verify search returns correct results for a valid pair query.
cases.preconditions: User is on the pair list / watchlist screen.
cases.expected: All pairs containing 'BTC' (BTCUSDT, BTCUSDC, BTCBUSD, etc.) displayed with correct prices and 24h change.

cases.title: Search is case-insensitive
cases.description: Verify search returns same results regardless of letter case.
cases.preconditions: User is on pair list screen.
cases.expected: Identical results for 'btc' and 'BTC'. Case does not affect search results.

cases.title: Clear search restores full pair list
cases.description: Verify clearing the search input restores the unfiltered pair list.
cases.preconditions: User has filtered list by typing 'ETH'.
steps: Clear the search input (click X or delete text)
cases.expected: Full unfiltered pair list is restored. All brokerages and pairs visible again.

cases.title: Add pair to watchlist via star icon
cases.description: Verify clicking the star icon adds a pair to the user's personal watchlist.
cases.preconditions: User is logged in. SOL/USDT is not yet starred.
cases.rawTestData: Pair: SOLUSDT | Brokerage: Binance | Status: unstarred
cases.expected: Star icon becomes filled. SOLUSDT appears in 'All Lists' starred/favourites filter.

cases.title: Remove pair from watchlist
cases.description: Verify clicking the filled star removes the pair from the watchlist.
cases.preconditions: SOLUSDT is currently starred in user's watchlist.
cases.expected: Star icon becomes empty/unfilled. SOLUSDT no longer appears in starred/favourites list.

cases.title: View 'My Lists' shows only starred pairs
cases.description: Verify switching to 'My Lists' / 'All Lists' filter shows only user's starred pairs.
cases.preconditions: User has at least 2 pairs starred (BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT).
steps: Click 'All Lists' toggle or my watchlist filter
cases.expected: Only BTCUSDT and ETHUSDT shown. No unstarred pairs visible in this filter.

cases.title: Watchlist persists after logout and login
cases.description: Verify watchlist is saved server-side and survives a full logout/login cycle.
cases.preconditions: User has BTCUSDT starred. User logs out.
steps: Open pair list → check My Lists
cases.expected: BTCUSDT is still starred after re-login. Watchlist data is persisted to user account.

cases.title: 24h change % in pair list matches chart header
cases.description: Verify 24h change % values in the pair list are accurate and consistent with chart header data.
cases.preconditions: User is on the pair list screen.
steps: Note BTC/USDT 24h change % in pair list
cases.expected: 24h change % in pair list matches the value shown in the chart header for the same pair.

cases.title: AI-generated tree matches described logic
cases.description: Verify the strategy tree generated by AI correctly implements the logic described in the prompt — not just a plausible-looking tree that doesn't match the intent.
cases.preconditions: User is in Strategy Builder with AI mode active

testData.prompt: 'Buy BTCUSDT when EMA 50 crosses above EMA 200 on 1H chart'

cases.rawTestData: Prompt: 'Buy BTCUSDT when EMA 50 crosses above EMA 200 on 1H chart'
cases.expected: AI generates a tree with correct EMA 50/200 crossover logic, correct symbol and timeframe. Condition blocks match the described intent.

cases.title: AI prompts for missing parameters before generating
cases.description: Verify AI does not silently assume missing parameters — it must ask the user to clarify before producing the tree.
cases.preconditions: User is in AI mode
cases.rawTestData: Vague prompt: 'Buy when RSI is low'
steps: Enter a vague prompt missing key details (symbol, period, threshold)
cases.expected: AI asks follow-up questions to clarify missing details (symbol, RSI period, threshold value) before generating the tree.

cases.title: AI-generated strategy executes correctly in backtest
cases.description: Verify the AI-generated strategy produces meaningful backtest results — not zero trades or errors.
cases.preconditions: AI-generated strategy tree is loaded in editor
steps: Run backtest with a defined period
cases.expected: Backtest completes without errors. Trade history shows entries/exits matching the defined condition. Results panel populates correctly.

cases.title: JSON export and re-import produces identical strategy tree
cases.description: Verify exported strategy JSON re-imports without loss of logic or parameters.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with multiple condition and action blocks is built
steps: Create new strategy and import the JSON
cases.expected: Re-imported strategy tree is identical to the original. All blocks, parameters, and logic connectors are preserved.

cases.title: Configure Left Operand as Bar Window (TradeClose) — condition saves correctly
cases.description: Verify that selecting Bar Window as an operand and configuring its parameters (Bar Type, Bar Property, Resolution, Interval, Symbol) saves and evaluates correctly.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block in the Alert/Strategy editor

testData.bar type: TradeBar, Bar Property: TradeClose, Resolution: 1H, Interval: 1, Symbol: BTCUSDT

cases.rawTestData: Bar Type: TradeBar, Bar Property: TradeClose, Resolution: 1H, Interval: 1, Symbol: BTCUSDT
steps: Click Left Operand → select Bar Window
Fill in: Bar Type=TradeBar, Bar Property=TradeClose, Resolution=1H, Interval=1, Symbol=BTCUSDT
Set operator (e.g. >) and Right Operand as Constant (70000)
cases.expected: Condition saves successfully. Bar Window parameters are persisted correctly. Block displays the configured operand details.

cases.title: Configure Left Operand as Indicator (e.g. RSI 14) — condition evaluates correctly
cases.description: Verify that an Indicator operand with correct period and resolution saves and is used correctly in condition evaluation.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block

testData.indicator: RSI, Period: 14, Resolution: 1H, compared to Constant: 30

cases.rawTestData: Indicator: RSI, Period: 14, Resolution: 1H, compared to Constant: 30
steps: Click Left Operand → select Indicator → choose RSI
cases.expected: Condition saves with RSI(14) on 1H correctly configured. In backtest/live, condition fires only when RSI drops below 30.

cases.title: Configure condition using CandlestickPattern operand
cases.description: Verify that CandlestickPattern can be selected as an operand and the pattern type is saved correctly.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block

testData.pattern type: e.g. Hammer or Engulfing (whichever is available)

cases.rawTestData: Pattern type: e.g. Hammer or Engulfing (whichever is available)
steps: Choose a pattern type and configure symbol/resolution
cases.expected: CandlestickPattern operand saves correctly. Pattern type and parameters are persisted. Condition block reflects the selection.

cases.title: Use Constant as operand — fixed numeric value accepted on both sides
cases.description: Verify Constant can be used as either left or right operand and the value is stored correctly.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block
steps: Set operator and Right Operand = Bar Window or Indicator
cases.expected: Constant value is accepted and persisted. Condition block shows the hardcoded value on the configured side.

cases.title: Switch condition logic connector from AND to OR
cases.description: Verify the logic connector between conditions can be toggled and the change is saved.
cases.preconditions: If Condition block with at least 2 sub-conditions exists
steps: Add If Condition block with 2 conditions
cases.expected: Logic connector toggles between AND and OR. The selected value is persisted after save. The condition block visually reflects the change.

cases.title: Multiple AND conditions — all must be true to trigger
cases.description: Verify a strategy with 2 AND conditions only executes when both conditions are simultaneously satisfied.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with 2 AND conditions built and backtested

testData.condition 1: RSI(14) < 30; Condition 2: Bar Window Close < 200-period MA

cases.rawTestData: Condition 1: RSI(14) < 30; Condition 2: Bar Window Close < 200-period MA
steps: Build strategy with 2 conditions joined by AND
Review trade entries — verify each entry satisfies both conditions
cases.expected: Every trade entry in backtest results occurs only when BOTH conditions are true at the same bar. No entries where only one condition is met.

cases.title: Multiple OR conditions — any one condition triggers execution
cases.description: Verify a strategy with OR logic executes when at least one condition is met.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with 2 OR conditions built and backtested

testData.condition 1: RSI(14) < 30; Condition 2: Bar Window Close > Upper Bollinger Band

cases.rawTestData: Condition 1: RSI(14) < 30; Condition 2: Bar Window Close > Upper Bollinger Band
steps: Build strategy with 2 conditions joined by OR
Review trade entries — verify entries occur when either condition alone is true
cases.expected: Trade entries occur whenever at least one condition is true. Entries are more frequent than with AND logic for the same conditions.

cases.title: Incomplete condition block (missing operand or value) → save blocked
cases.description: Verify that an unfinished condition block prevents saving or running the strategy.
cases.preconditions: User has added an If Condition block but left operand or value empty
steps: Configure Left Operand but leave Right Operand empty
cases.expected: System displays a validation error pointing to the incomplete block. Save/Run is blocked until the condition is fully configured.

cases.title: Place Order (Single) — correct quantity and entry price in backtest
cases.description: Verify a single market order fires at the correct price and quantity when the condition triggers.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with a single Place Order block connected to an If Condition
cases.rawTestData: Symbol: BTCUSDT, Quantity: 0.1, Order type: Market
steps: Build strategy: If RSI<30 → Place Order (Market, 0.1 BTC)
cases.expected: Each trade entry shows correct symbol, quantity (0.1), and execution at market price of the trigger bar.

cases.title: Bracket Order — Take Profit fills correctly before Stop Loss when price rises
cases.description: Verify TP fills and SL is cancelled when price moves favourably.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with Bracket Order (TP and SL set)
steps: Build strategy with Bracket Order (TP=+2%, SL=-1%)
Run backtest on a period with clear uptrend
Find a trade where price hit TP
cases.expected: TP order fills at the correct price. Corresponding SL order is cancelled after TP fills. Trade PnL is positive.

cases.title: Bracket Order — Stop Loss fires and TP is cancelled when price drops
cases.description: Verify SL fires and TP is cancelled when price moves against the position.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with Bracket Order (TP and SL set)
steps: Build strategy with Bracket Order (TP=+2%, SL=-1%)
Run backtest on a period with a drop after entry
Find a trade where SL was hit
cases.expected: SL order fills at the correct price. TP order is cancelled after SL fires. Trade PnL reflects the -1% loss.

cases.title: Place Order quantity as percentage — position size scales with equity
cases.description: Verify that a percentage-based quantity calculates the correct position size based on current equity.
cases.preconditions: Strategy using percentage quantity (e.g. 10% of equity)
cases.rawTestData: Initial equity: 10,000 USD, Order: 10% of equity
steps: Configure Place Order: quantity=10% of equity
cases.expected: First order quantity equals 10% of 10,000 USD = 1,000 USD worth of the asset. Subsequent orders scale correctly as equity changes.

cases.subFeature: Liquidate, Cancel, Set Holding, Stop Strategy
cases.title: Liquidate closes 100% of open position — not a partial close
cases.description: Verify Liquidate block closes the entire open position, not just part of it.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with an open position and a Liquidate block on exit condition
steps: Build strategy: Entry → If exit condition → Liquidate
cases.expected: When exit condition fires, the full open position is closed at market. No residual position remains. Trade PnL reflects full close.

cases.subFeature: Liquidate, Cancel, Set Holding, Stop Strategy
cases.title: Cancel Order prevents a pending limit order from filling after price moves past limit
cases.description: Verify Cancel Order block correctly voids a pending order before it fills.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with a pending limit order and a Cancel block triggered by a condition

testData._0: Limit order set 1% below current price

cases.rawTestData: Limit order set 1% below current price
steps: Build strategy: Place Limit Order → If cancellation condition → Cancel Order
Run backtest where cancel condition fires before limit is reached
cases.expected: Limit order is cancelled before filling. No trade entry recorded for that bar. Order does not fill even if price later reaches the limit.

cases.subFeature: Liquidate, Cancel, Set Holding, Stop Strategy
cases.title: Set Holding rebalances to target percentage — buys or sells the correct delta
cases.description: Verify Set Holding calculates and executes the correct delta order to reach the target allocation.
cases.preconditions: Strategy using Set Holding block with a defined target %
cases.rawTestData: Current position: 5% of equity; Target: 20% of equity
steps: Build strategy with Set Holding (target=20% of equity)
Run backtest from a state where current holding is 5%
cases.expected: System places a buy order for the delta (15% of equity) to reach the 20% target. If already over-allocated, a sell order is placed for the excess.

cases.subFeature: Liquidate, Cancel, Set Holding, Stop Strategy
cases.title: Stop Strategy halts all subsequent execution — no further orders after trigger
cases.description: Verify that once Stop Strategy fires, no further condition checks or orders are executed.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with a Stop Strategy block triggered by a loss threshold
steps: Build strategy with Stop Strategy block: If Net PnL < -5% → Stop Strategy
Run backtest on a volatile period
Check orders after the stop trigger bar
cases.expected: After Stop Strategy fires, no new orders are placed for the remainder of the backtest period. Strategy effectively halts.

cases.subFeature: Liquidate, Cancel, Set Holding, Stop Strategy
cases.title: Advanced action does NOT fire when its parent condition is false
cases.description: Verify action blocks are only executed when their parent If Condition is true.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with an action block nested inside an If Condition
steps: Verify Liquidate only fires on bars where RSI > 70
cases.expected: Liquidate (or other action) fires only on bars where the parent condition is true. No action on bars where the condition is false.

cases.title: Updating a strategy while a paper trade is running — behaviour is defined and safe
cases.description: Verify that editing a strategy mid-run does not corrupt the running paper trade or cause unexpected orders.
cases.preconditions: A strategy is actively running in Paper Trade mode
steps: Navigate to Strategy Builder and edit the strategy (e.g. change a condition value)
cases.expected: System either applies the update safely or shows a clear warning about editing a live strategy. No duplicate orders, crashes, or undefined behaviour.

cases.title: Deleting a strategy that has active executions is blocked or requires confirmation
cases.description: Verify the system prevents silent deletion of strategies with active runs.
cases.preconditions: Strategy has an active Paper Trade or Live Trade
steps: Attempt to delete a strategy that is currently running
cases.expected: System either blocks deletion with a clear message or shows a confirmation dialog warning that active executions exist. No silent deletion.

cases.title: Cloned strategy is fully independent from the original
cases.description: Verify edits to a cloned strategy do not affect the original, and vice versa.
cases.preconditions: A strategy has been cloned
steps: Modify the clone (change a condition value)
cases.expected: Original strategy is unchanged after editing the clone. Both strategies maintain independent configurations.

cases.title: Backtest PnL matches the sum of individual trade PnLs
cases.description: Verify the total Net Profit shown in results equals the sum of all individual trade PnLs.
cases.preconditions: Backtest has completed with multiple trades
steps: Note the Net Profit in the overview
Open Trades panel and sum all individual PnL values
cases.expected: Sum of individual trade PnLs equals the Net Profit shown in the overview (allowing for rounding). No unexplained discrepancy.

cases.title: Backtest is deterministic — same inputs produce same results every run
cases.description: Verify running the same backtest twice produces identical results.
cases.preconditions: Strategy and backtest parameters are fixed

testData._0: Same symbol, timeframe, date range, initial capital

cases.rawTestData: Same symbol, timeframe, date range, initial capital
steps: Run backtest and record Net Profit, trade count, Sharpe Ratio
Run the same backtest again without changing anything
cases.expected: Both runs produce identical Net Profit, trade count, and all metrics. Backtest engine is not randomised.

cases.title: Backtest with initial capital insufficient for first order — handled gracefully
cases.description: Verify the system handles underfunded backtests without crashing.
cases.preconditions: Strategy requires more capital than the initial balance
cases.rawTestData: Initial capital: 10 USD; Order: 1 BTC at market
cases.expected: System displays a clear error or skips the order with a log entry. Backtest does not crash. Results show zero trades or an appropriate error message.

cases.title: Backtest period with zero signals — results show empty trade history, not errors
cases.description: Verify a strategy that generates no signals over a period returns an empty result cleanly.
cases.preconditions: Strategy with very restrictive conditions unlikely to trigger
cases.expected: Results panel shows 0 trades. No errors, null values, or crashes. Metrics show 0 or N/A as appropriate.

cases.title: Backtest Sharpe Ratio calculation is consistent with trade data
cases.description: Verify the reported Sharpe Ratio is plausible given the win rate and return data.
cases.preconditions: Backtest with at least 10 trades completed
steps: Note Sharpe Ratio, Return %, Win Rate
Cross-check: high win rate + positive return should yield Sharpe > 1
cases.expected: Sharpe Ratio is directionally consistent with the return and volatility data. Positive-return strategies with good win rates show Sharpe > 0.

cases.title: Backtest result after publishing to Algo Hub — metrics match original backtest
cases.description: Verify metrics displayed in Algo Hub match the backtest that was published.
cases.preconditions: Strategy has been backtested and published to Algo Hub
steps: Run backtest and record key metrics (Net Profit, Win Rate, Sharpe, Drawdown)
Open strategy in Algo Hub and compare metrics
cases.expected: All metrics in Algo Hub match the original backtest results exactly. No rounding or display differences beyond 2 decimal places.

cases.title: Paper trade places virtual order exactly when live condition fires — not on delay
cases.description: Verify the paper trade order timestamp matches the bar where the condition triggered.
cases.preconditions: Strategy is running in Paper Trade mode
steps: Check order timestamp vs condition trigger bar time
cases.expected: Virtual order is placed on the same bar the condition fired. No bar delay. Order entry price matches the bar's relevant price (open/close depending on config).

cases.title: Paper trade equity updates correctly after each virtual order fill
cases.description: Verify the equity balance in the paper trade updates after each trade.
cases.preconditions: Paper trade is running with at least one completed trade
steps: Let a condition trigger and fill
cases.expected: Equity updates correctly: decreases by position cost on entry, increases/decreases by PnL on exit.

cases.title: Paper trade does not generate any real orders on the connected brokerage
cases.description: Verify paper trade is fully isolated from real order execution.
cases.preconditions: Brokerage account is connected; paper trade is running
steps: Start paper trade on a strategy
Let conditions fire and virtual orders execute
Check real brokerage account for any orders
cases.expected: Zero real orders appear on the brokerage account. Paper trade is completely isolated from live execution.

cases.title: Paper trade result is consistent with backtest over the same period
cases.description: Verify paper trade PnL approximately matches a backtest run over the same period and conditions.
cases.preconditions: Strategy has both a completed backtest and paper trade over the same date range
steps: Note backtest results for a specific period
Run paper trade over the same period
Compare Net Profit and trade count
cases.expected: Paper trade results are within a reasonable margin of backtest results. Significant discrepancy (>10%) is flagged for investigation.

cases.title: Live trade places a real order on the brokerage when condition fires
cases.description: Verify a real order appears on the brokerage exchange when the strategy condition triggers.
cases.preconditions: Brokerage account is connected with sufficient balance; live trade is running
steps: Wait for condition to fire (or use a condition likely to trigger quickly in test)
cases.expected: A real order appears on the brokerage with the correct symbol, quantity, and type. Order timestamp matches the condition trigger time.

cases.title: Live trade with insufficient brokerage balance — order failure handled without crash
cases.description: Verify the system handles insufficient funds gracefully during live execution.
cases.preconditions: Brokerage account balance is below the required order size
cases.rawTestData: Balance: 1 USD; Order: 1 BTC at market
steps: Connect an account with near-zero balance
cases.expected: System logs an insufficient balance error. No crash or unhandled exception. BeeTrade dashboard shows the failed order with a clear error message.

cases.title: Stopping live trade does not automatically close open real positions
cases.description: Verify stopping the strategy leaves existing brokerage positions open.
cases.preconditions: Live trade has an open real position on the brokerage
steps: Start live trade and let a position open
Stop the live trade from BeeTrade
cases.expected: Open position remains on the brokerage after stopping. No automatic close order is sent. User is responsible for manually managing the position.

cases.title: Live trade PnL in BeeTrade dashboard matches brokerage PnL reporting
cases.description: Verify the PnL shown in BeeTrade matches the actual PnL on the brokerage side.
cases.preconditions: Live trade has completed at least one round-trip trade
steps: Complete a buy + sell cycle via live trade
Check the same trade on the brokerage
cases.expected: PnL values match within fee rounding tolerance. Any discrepancy is explainable by exchange fees or timing differences.

cases.title: Live tab shows all active bots with mini charts
cases.description: Verify that the Live tab displays every running bot as a card with a mini chart preview.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 bot is running (Paper or Live mode)
steps: Observe bot cards in the right panel
cases.expected: Each running bot displays its symbol, badge (PAPER/LIVE), PnL%, and a mini chart. No blank cards.

cases.title: Portfolio stats are accurate (Net Profit, Total Equity, Win Rate, etc.)
cases.description: Verify the left-panel aggregate stats correctly reflect the sum of all running bots.
cases.preconditions: Multiple bots running with trade activity

testData._0: Known expected totals from individual bot detail pages

cases.rawTestData: Known expected totals from individual bot detail pages
steps: Note Net Profit, Total Equity, Win Rate, Max Drawdown, Avg Win/Loss, Profit Factor
cases.expected: All aggregate stats match the sum/average of individual bot values. Values update without page refresh.

cases.title: Best / Worst strategy labels update correctly
cases.description: Verify the Best and Worst labels in the portfolio summary point to the correct bots.
cases.preconditions: At least 2 bots with different PnL values

testData._0: Bots with clearly different Net Profit values

cases.rawTestData: Bots with clearly different Net Profit values
steps: Note which bot has highest and lowest Net Profit
cases.expected: Best label shows the bot with highest Net Profit. Worst shows the bot with lowest (or most negative) Net Profit.

cases.title: Strategy list shows correct Net Profit & PnL% per bot
cases.description: Verify the bottom strategy list rows display accurate per-bot Net Profit and PnL% figures.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 bot running
steps: Look at the strategy list at the bottom of the left panel
Compare Net Profit and PnL% values with bot detail pages
cases.expected: Each row's Net Profit and PnL% values match the corresponding bot's detail page. Positive values shown in green, negative in red.

cases.title: No active bots → empty state shown in Live tab
cases.description: Verify the Live tab handles zero running bots gracefully without errors.
cases.preconditions: All bots are stopped or no bots exist
cases.expected: Empty state message is shown (e.g. 'No active bots'). No broken charts or null values displayed.

cases.title: Activity tab lists all bots (Running + Stopped)
cases.description: Verify that the Activity tab shows both currently running bots and all historical/stopped bots.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 running and 1 stopped bot exist
steps: Check that the list shows bots with 'Running' and 'Stopped' statuses
cases.expected: Both running and stopped bots appear in the list. Record count matches the displayed result count.

cases.title: Bot row displays correct values: Equity, Net Profit, Return %, Period, Duration
cases.description: Verify each row in the Activity list shows accurate data columns.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 bot with trade history

testData._0: Known values from bot detail page

cases.rawTestData: Known values from bot detail page
steps: Select a bot and compare its row values (Equity, Net Profit, Return %, Period, Duration) against its Detail page
cases.expected: All column values match the bot's detail page. No '--' or N/A in rows where data should exist.

cases.title: Filter by Type (e.g. only GRID bots)
cases.description: Verify the Type filter returns only bots of the selected type.
cases.preconditions: Multiple bot types exist in the list
cases.expected: Only GRID bots are shown. Result count updates. Other bot types are hidden.

cases.title: Filter by Status: Running vs Stopped
cases.description: Verify the Status filter correctly filters bots by their current status.
cases.preconditions: Both Running and Stopped bots exist
steps: Set STATUS = Running → verify only running bots shown
Set STATUS = Stopped → verify only stopped bots shown
cases.expected: Each status filter shows only matching bots. Counter reflects filtered count.

cases.title: Date range filter shows only bots within selected period
cases.description: Verify the date range picker filters bots by their start/run period.
cases.preconditions: Bots with different start dates exist

testData._0: Select a date range that covers some but not all bots

cases.rawTestData: Select a date range that covers some but not all bots
steps: Click 'Select date range' and pick a specific period
cases.expected: Only bots whose period overlaps with the selected date range are displayed.

cases.title: Sort by Newest / Oldest
cases.description: Verify the sort toggle correctly reorders bots by creation/start date.
cases.preconditions: Multiple bots with different start dates
cases.expected: Newest: most recently started bot appears first. Oldest: earliest started bot appears first.

cases.title: Clear filters → full list restored
cases.description: Verify the Clear button resets all active filters and shows the full bot list.
cases.preconditions: At least one filter is active
steps: Apply Type = DCA and Status = Running
cases.expected: All filters are removed. Full unfiltered bot list is restored. Clear button disappears.

cases.title: Stop a running bot → status changes, removed from Live tab
cases.description: Verify stopping a bot updates its status and removes it from the Live view.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 bot in Running status
steps: Check Activity tab status and Live tab
cases.expected: Bot status changes to Stopped. It is removed from the Live tab charts and stats. Portfolio summary updates.

cases.title: Click Details → bot detail page opens with correct info
cases.description: Verify the Details button navigates to a bot detail page with accurate information.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 bot exists in the list
cases.expected: Bot detail page opens. Shows correct symbol, type, status, equity, P&L, and trade history matching the Activity list row.

cases.title: Strategy cards load with correct metrics
cases.description: Verify each strategy card displays all key metrics correctly in Grid view.
cases.preconditions: User is on Algo Hub page, strategies exist in the community list
steps: Observe strategy cards in Grid view
Check each card for: PNL%, USD amount, mini chart, Period, Win Rate, Sharpe Ratio, Drawdown
cases.expected: Every card shows PNL%, USD value, mini chart, Period, Win Rate, Sharpe Ratio, and Drawdown. No blank or '--' values where data exists.

cases.title: Sort by Newest & pagination navigate correctly
cases.description: Verify sort order and page navigation return the correct set of strategies.
cases.preconditions: More than 16 strategies published in Algo Hub
steps: Click next page → verify new set of strategies
Click previous → verify original set returns
cases.expected: Newest sort shows most recently published strategy first. Pagination correctly loads next/previous pages. Page count (e.g. 1/2) is accurate.

cases.title: Switch between Algo Hub and My Published tabs
cases.description: Verify each tab shows the correct scope of strategies.
cases.preconditions: User has at least 1 published strategy; community has other strategies
steps: Open Algo Hub tab → note strategies shown
Click 'My Published' tab → check list changes
cases.expected: Algo Hub shows all community strategies. My Published shows only the current user's strategies. Switching tabs updates the list without page reload errors.

cases.title: Keyword search returns matching strategies
cases.description: Verify that typing a keyword and pressing Enter filters strategies by name.
cases.preconditions: Strategies with known names exist in Algo Hub
cases.expected: Only strategies with 'EMA' in their name are shown. Result count updates accordingly.

cases.title: Search with no match → empty state shown
cases.description: Verify a search that returns no results shows a clear empty state rather than breaking the UI.
cases.preconditions: User is on Algo Hub
steps: Type 'xyznotexist999' in search and press Enter
cases.expected: Empty state message is displayed (e.g. 'No strategies found'). No errors, blank cards, or crashes.

cases.title: Filter by single metric returns correct results
cases.description: Verify that applying one filter condition correctly narrows the strategy list.
cases.preconditions: Strategies with varied metrics exist
cases.expected: Only strategies with Drawdown below 20% are shown. Result count is less than or equal to total. Strategies exceeding the threshold are hidden.

cases.title: Multiple filter conditions combine correctly
cases.description: Verify that adding a second filter condition applies AND logic.
cases.preconditions: Strategies with varied metrics exist
cases.rawTestData: Filter 1: Net Profit (%) > 10; Filter 2: Win Rate (%) > 50
cases.expected: Only strategies meeting BOTH conditions are returned. Result count is fewer than or equal to each individual filter result.

cases.title: Clear all filters restores full strategy list
cases.description: Verify the Clear All button removes all active filters and shows the unfiltered list.
cases.preconditions: At least one filter is active
cases.expected: All filters are removed. Full community strategy list is restored. Result count matches the unfiltered total.

cases.title: Show details opens full strategy detail page with overview metrics
cases.description: Verify clicking 'Show details' navigates to the detail page with all key metrics present.
cases.preconditions: At least 1 strategy exists in Algo Hub
cases.expected: Detail page opens. Overview panel displays: Total Orders, Avg Win, Avg Loss, Compounding Annual Return, Drawdown, Expectancy, Start Equity, End Equity, Net Profit, Sharpe Ratio.

cases.title: Trading Chart renders with Buy/Sell signals and all Analytics tabs load
cases.description: Verify the Trading Chart and all 5 analytics tabs display data without errors.
cases.preconditions: Strategy detail page is open
steps: On strategy detail page, open Trading Chart tab
Verify chart renders with Buy/Sell signal markers
Click each tab: Equity, Capacity, Exposure, Drawdown, Benchmark
cases.expected: Trading Chart shows candlestick chart with Buy/Sell markers and indicators. All 5 analytics tabs load charts/data without errors or blank screens.

cases.title: Execution panel shows Trades, Orders, Order Events with PnL data
cases.description: Verify the right-side execution panel lists trade-level data correctly.
cases.preconditions: Strategy has completed trades (backtest period with activity)
steps: Review the execution panel on the right
Switch between Trades, Orders, Order Events tabs
cases.expected: Each panel tab loads a table with relevant columns. Trades show Symbol, PnL, Entry/Exit. Orders show quantity, price, type. No empty tables where data should exist.

cases.title: Clone from strategy card (list view) opens copy in Strategy Builder
cases.description: Verify the clone icon on a strategy card creates a copy and opens it in Strategy Builder.
cases.preconditions: User is on Algo Hub list view
steps: Click the Clone icon on any strategy card
cases.expected: System navigates to Strategy Builder with a copy of the selected strategy loaded. Strategy name, indicators, and logic match the original.

cases.title: Clone from strategy detail page produces the same result
cases.description: Verify that cloning from the detail page works identically to cloning from the list.
cases.preconditions: User is on a strategy detail page
steps: Compare the copy to the original
cases.expected: Strategy Builder opens with a copy that matches the original strategy config. Both entry points (card and detail) produce identical results.

cases.title: Cloned strategy can be run as Backtest, Paper Trade, or Live Trade
cases.description: Verify all 3 run modes are available after cloning a strategy.
cases.preconditions: A cloned strategy is open in Strategy Builder
steps: In Strategy Builder, check available run options
Verify Paper Trade and Live Trade options are also accessible
cases.expected: All 3 modes — Backtest, Paper Trade, Live Trade — are available and can be initiated from the cloned strategy without errors.

cases.title: My Published tab shows only current user's strategies
cases.description: Verify the My Published tab is scoped to the logged-in user's published strategies only.
cases.preconditions: User has published at least 1 strategy; other users have also published strategies
cases.expected: Only strategies published by the current logged-in user appear. Other users' strategies are not shown.

cases.title: User's published strategy is visible in community Algo Hub list
cases.description: Verify that after publishing, a strategy appears in the public Algo Hub list for all users.
cases.preconditions: User has published a strategy from Strategy Builder
steps: Publish a strategy from Strategy Builder
Search for the strategy by name
cases.expected: The published strategy appears in the Algo Hub list and is discoverable by other users via search.

cases.title: Describe alert in natural language → AI proposes condition tree
cases.description: Verify AI correctly interprets a plain-language description and returns a valid If-condition tree.
cases.preconditions: User is in Alert module with AI mode active

testData.prompt: 'Create an alert for BTCUSDT when RSI (14) is below 30 on the 1-hour chart'

cases.rawTestData: Prompt: 'Create an alert for BTCUSDT when RSI (14) is below 30 on the 1-hour chart'
cases.expected: AI returns a proposal with a text summary and a visual If-condition tree using correct operand types. Parameters (symbol, indicator, value, timeframe) match the prompt.

cases.title: AI proposal with incomplete info → AI asks for clarification
cases.description: Verify AI requests missing details before generating a tree rather than assuming defaults silently.
cases.preconditions: User is in AI mode

testData.vague prompt: 'Alert me when BTC moves a lot'

cases.rawTestData: Vague prompt: 'Alert me when BTC moves a lot'
steps: Enter a vague prompt missing key parameters
cases.expected: AI responds with follow-up questions asking for clarification on missing details (threshold, direction, timeframe) before generating the condition tree.

cases.title: Apply AI-generated alert → condition tree loads into editor
cases.description: Verify clicking 'Apply Alert' correctly populates the Alert Editor with the AI-generated condition blocks.
cases.preconditions: AI has returned a valid alert proposal
cases.expected: Alert Editor populates with the condition tree from the AI proposal. All blocks, operand values, and logic connectors are correctly loaded.

cases.title: Create alert with all required fields filled → saved successfully
cases.description: Verify a fully configured manual alert saves without errors.
cases.preconditions: User is on Alerts page

testData.name: 'BTC RSI Alert', Brokerage: BINANCE, Notify Via: Web + Email, Active: ON

cases.rawTestData: Name: 'BTC RSI Alert', Brokerage: BINANCE, Notify Via: Web + Email, Active: ON
cases.expected: Alert is saved and appears in the Alert List with status Active. All configured fields are persisted correctly.

cases.title: Create alert with expiration date → auto-deactivates when expired
cases.description: Verify alert with a set expiration automatically becomes inactive after the expiration datetime.
cases.preconditions: Alert is created with a near-future expiration time

testData.expiration: set to 2 minutes from creation time

cases.rawTestData: Expiration: set to 2 minutes from creation time
steps: Create alert with expiration set to 2 minutes ahead
Wait for expiration time to pass
cases.expected: After expiration, alert status changes to Inactive automatically. Alert no longer triggers notifications. Expiration time is shown in the alert details.

cases.title: Create alert without required field (Name or Brokerage) → validation error
cases.description: Verify the system blocks saving an incomplete alert and shows a clear error.
cases.preconditions: User is creating a new alert
steps: Fill in Name but skip Brokerage
cases.expected: System shows a validation error on the empty required field. Alert is not saved. Error message clearly indicates which field is missing.

cases.title: Configure Left Operand as Bar Window (TradeClose) — condition saves correctly
cases.description: Verify Bar Window operand with all parameters (Bar Type, Bar Property, Resolution, Interval, Symbol) saves correctly.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block in Alert Editor

testData.bar type: TradeBar, Bar Property: TradeClose, Resolution: 1H, Interval: 1, Symbol: BTCUSDT

cases.rawTestData: Bar Type: TradeBar, Bar Property: TradeClose, Resolution: 1H, Interval: 1, Symbol: BTCUSDT
steps: Click Left Operand → select Bar Window
Set: Bar Type=TradeBar, Bar Property=TradeClose, Resolution=1H, Interval=1, Symbol=BTCUSDT
cases.expected: Condition saves with all Bar Window parameters. Block displays the operand configuration correctly. No validation errors.

cases.title: Configure Left Operand as Indicator (e.g. RSI 14) — condition evaluates correctly
cases.description: Verify Indicator operand saves with correct period and resolution, and the alert fires based on indicator value.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block
steps: Click Left Operand → select Indicator → choose RSI
cases.expected: Condition saves with RSI(14) on 1H correctly. Alert fires when RSI drops below 30. Parameters persist after save.

cases.title: Configure condition using CandlestickPattern operand
cases.description: Verify CandlestickPattern operand can be selected and pattern type is saved correctly.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block
steps: Choose a pattern type and configure symbol/resolution
cases.expected: Pattern type and parameters save correctly. Condition block reflects the selected pattern. Alert fires only when that candlestick pattern appears.

cases.title: Use Constant as operand — fixed numeric value accepted
cases.description: Verify Constant can be used as an operand and the numeric value is stored correctly.
cases.preconditions: User is building a condition block
steps: Set one side as Constant and enter 50000
cases.expected: Constant value is accepted and displayed in the condition block. Value persists after save.

cases.title: Switch condition logic connector from AND to OR
cases.description: Verify the logic connector between conditions can be toggled and saved.
cases.preconditions: If Condition block with at least 2 sub-conditions exists
steps: Add If Condition block with 2 conditions
Edit → switch to OR → save again
cases.expected: Connector toggles between AND and OR. Selection is persisted after save. Visual indicator in editor reflects the change.

cases.title: Multiple AND conditions — alert fires only when all conditions are met simultaneously
cases.description: Verify AND logic requires all conditions to be true at the same time.
cases.preconditions: Alert with 2 AND conditions configured and active
cases.rawTestData: Condition 1: RSI(14) < 30; Condition 2: Bar Window Close < 65000
steps: Monitor — alert should only fire when BOTH conditions are true
cases.expected: Notification fires only on bars/moments where both RSI < 30 AND Close < 65000 are simultaneously true. No false alerts from a single condition.

cases.title: Multiple OR conditions — alert fires when any one condition is met
cases.description: Verify OR logic triggers the alert when at least one condition is true.
cases.preconditions: Alert with 2 OR conditions configured and active
steps: Monitor — alert should fire when either condition is true
cases.expected: Notification fires when either RSI drops below 30 OR rises above 70. Fires more frequently than a single condition alone.

cases.title: Incomplete condition block (missing operand or value) → save blocked
cases.description: Verify saving is prevented when a condition block is not fully configured.
cases.preconditions: User has added an If Condition block but left an operand empty
steps: Configure Left Operand but leave Right Operand empty
cases.expected: System shows a validation error on the incomplete block. Alert is not saved until all operand fields are filled.

cases.title: Edit existing condition block — changes are reflected in the alert
cases.description: Verify modifying a condition block in an existing alert updates it correctly.
cases.preconditions: Alert with an existing If Condition block exists
steps: Open an existing alert in the editor
Click on the condition block and change the Constant value
cases.expected: Updated value is saved and displayed in the condition block. Alert behaviour reflects the new threshold going forward.

cases.title: Toggle alert ON/OFF → status updates immediately and persists
cases.description: Verify the active toggle correctly activates/deactivates an alert and the state is saved.
cases.preconditions: At least one alert exists in the list
steps: Find an Active alert in the list
cases.expected: Status changes immediately on toggle. State persists after page refresh. Inactive alerts do not fire notifications.

cases.title: Edit existing alert — logic and setup changes are saved correctly
cases.description: Verify changes to an existing alert's configuration are persisted.
cases.preconditions: An existing alert is open in the editor

testData._0: Change alert name and add a second condition

cases.rawTestData: Change alert name and add a second condition
cases.expected: Updated name and new condition block are saved. Alert list reflects the new name. Updated logic takes effect for future triggers.

cases.title: Delete an alert → permanently removed from list
cases.description: Verify deleted alerts are removed and do not reappear.
cases.preconditions: At least one alert exists
steps: Select an alert from the list
cases.expected: Alert is permanently removed from the list. No ghost entry remains. Deleted alert no longer triggers notifications.

cases.title: Expired alert auto-deactivates → can be reactivated by editing expiration
cases.description: Verify expired alerts deactivate automatically and can be brought back by updating the expiration.
cases.preconditions: An alert with a past expiration date exists
steps: Edit the expiration to a future date or set No Expiration
cases.expected: Alert becomes Active again after expiration is updated. Notifications resume. Expiration date reflects the new value.

cases.title: Active alert condition met → Web notification appears on BeeTrade
cases.description: Verify a real-time web notification is delivered when an active alert's condition is satisfied.
cases.preconditions: Alert is Active with Notify Via = Web; condition is likely to trigger
steps: Create and activate alert with Web notification
Wait for condition to be met (or set a condition currently true)
cases.expected: Web notification appears in the BeeTrade interface promptly when the condition is met. Notification includes alert name and trigger details.

cases.title: Active alert with Email → notification sent to registered email address
cases.description: Verify email notification is delivered to the user's registered address when condition fires.
cases.preconditions: Alert is Active with Notify Via = Email
steps: Create and activate alert with Email notification
cases.expected: Email notification is received with correct alert name and condition details. Email is sent to the account's registered address.

cases.title: Inactive alert condition met → no notification sent
cases.description: Verify alerts that are toggled off do not fire even when conditions are met.
cases.preconditions: Alert is Inactive; condition is known to be currently true
steps: Create alert with a condition currently true (e.g. RSI > 0)
cases.expected: No web or email notification is sent despite the condition being true. Inactive status fully suppresses alert firing.
